root_text.an6.32:1.1: Atha kho aññatarā devatā abhikkantāya rattiyā abhikkantavaṇṇā kevalakappaṁ jetavanaṁ obhāsetvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi. Ekamantaṁ ṭhitā kho sā devatā bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an6.32:2.3: Satthugāravatā, dhammagāravatā, saṅghagāravatā, sikkhāgāravatā, appamādagāravatā, paṭisanthāragāravatā—
root_text.an6.32:2.4: ime kho, bhante, cha dhammā bhikkhuno aparihānāya saṁvattantī”ti.
root_text.an6.32:2.7: Atha kho sā devatā “samanuñño me satthā”ti bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā tatthevantaradhāyi.
root_text.an6.32:3.1: Atha kho bhagavā tassā rattiyā accayena bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.an6.32:3.2: “imaṁ, bhikkhave, rattiṁ aññatarā devatā abhikkantāya rattiyā abhikkantavaṇṇā kevalakappaṁ jetavanaṁ obhāsetvā yenāhaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā maṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi. Ekamantaṁ ṭhitā kho, bhikkhave, sā devatā maṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an6.32:3.5: Satthugāravatā, dhammagāravatā, saṅghagāravatā, sikkhāgāravatā, appamādagāravatā, paṭisanthāragāravatā—
root_text.an6.32:3.6: ime kho, bhante, cha dhammā bhikkhuno aparihānāya saṁvattantī’ti.
root_text.an6.32:3.8: Idaṁ vatvā maṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā tatthevantaradhāyīti.

translation_text.an6.32:1.1: Then, late at night, a glorious deity, lighting up the entire Jeta’s Grove, went up to the Buddha, bowed, stood to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.an6.32:2.1: “Sir, these six things don’t lead to the decline of a mendicant.
translation_text.an6.32:2.3: Respect for the Teacher, for the teaching, for the Saṅgha, for the training, for diligence, and for hospitality.
translation_text.an6.32:2.4: These six things don’t lead to the decline of a mendicant.”
translation_text.an6.32:2.7: Then that deity, knowing that the teacher approved, bowed, and respectfully circled the Buddha, keeping him on his right, before vanishing right there.
translation_text.an6.32:3.1: Then, when the night had passed, the Buddha told the mendicants all that had happened, adding:
translation_text.an6.32:4.1: “Respect for the Teacher and the teaching,
translation_text.an6.32:4.2: and keen respect for the Saṅgha;
translation_text.an6.32:4.6: and has drawn near to extinguishment.”
